name: Alpha Momentum Pro
description: Uses dual moving average crossover with configurable fast and slow windows. When the fast MA crosses above the slow MA, the strategy enters long; when it crosses below, it exits.
thesis: A momentum strategy that adjusts its lookback window to follow persistent market strength without relying on a fixed setting.
bestFor: Investors looking for equity exposure with a rules-based momentum tilt.
mayNotSuit: Investors who want low turnover or strong protection in sharp reversals.
cardFooterMicrocopy: Momentum strategies can perform strongly in sustained trends and struggle when leadership changes quickly.

name: Trend Tracker
description: Buy and hold strategy that follows the prevailing market trend. Low complexity, low turnover, suitable for passive investors seeking broad market exposure.
thesis: A simple trend-following strategy that stays with broad market direction rather than trying to predict short-term reversals.
bestFor: Investors who want a straightforward rules-based trend approach.
mayNotSuit: Investors who interpret "low risk" as low drawdown in every market environment.
cardFooterMicrocopy: Trend-following can lag in choppy markets and still experience meaningful drawdowns during unstable transitions.

name: Bollinger Reversion
description: Enters positions when price touches the lower Bollinger Band (oversold) and exits at the middle band or upper band. Configurable window and band width.
thesis: A mean-reversion strategy that looks for temporary moves away from a normal trading range and positions for a return towards it.
bestFor: Investors looking for a different return profile from pure trend strategies.
mayNotSuit: Investors who prefer simple long-only trend exposure or very low trading activity.
cardFooterMicrocopy: Mean-reversion often works best in range-bound markets and can struggle when strong trends persist.

name: RSI Momentum
description: Uses the Relative Strength Index to time entries at oversold levels and exits at overbought levels. Higher risk due to concentrated positioning.
thesis: A signal-driven strategy using RSI thresholds to react to oversold and overbought conditions.
bestFor: Investors who want a more tactical and indicator-based strategy.
mayNotSuit: Investors looking for steadier long-term behaviour or stronger historical risk-adjusted performance.
cardFooterMicrocopy: Oscillator-based strategies can be sensitive to parameter choices and may whipsaw in noisy markets.

name: Balanced Growth
description: Combines equity and bond exposure using SPY and TLT, rebalanced quarterly. Targets steady growth with reduced drawdowns through diversification.
thesis: A diversified multi-asset strategy designed to spread risk across different exposures rather than depend on a single market.
bestFor: Investors who want a steadier, more diversified starting point.
mayNotSuit: Investors seeking concentrated upside from one style or one asset class.
cardFooterMicrocopy: Diversification can reduce concentration risk, but it does not remove the possibility of losses.

name: Gold Hedge
description: Allocates to gold (GLD) as an inflation hedge and tail-risk protection layer. Simple buy-and-hold with periodic rebalancing.
thesis: A defensive allocation overlay using gold exposure to help offset periods when risk assets are under pressure.
bestFor: Investors looking to reduce portfolio concentration to equities.
mayNotSuit: Investors expecting high growth from a defensive allocation on its own.
cardFooterMicrocopy: Defensive allocations can help with diversification, but they may lag when equity markets are strong.
